zh.lifeSummary: 當Ruth Mary Covey於1901年10月12日出生在美國紐約州奧瑟格奧瑟格時，她的父親Silvanus Hartwell Covey43歲，母親Carrie A Utter48歲。她於1925年8月15日與Roy Captain White在美國華盛頓州克拉克溫哥華結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她於1905年住在美國紐約州奧瑟格德卡度。她去世於1994年11月28日在美國華盛頓州可利吉塔特懷特 賽門享年93歲，埋葬在美國華盛頓州斯卡馬尼亞卡森。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ruth Mary Covey于1901年10月12日出生在美国纽约州奧瑟格奧瑟格时，她的父亲Silvanus Hartwell Covey43岁，母亲Carrie A Utter48岁。她于1925年8月15日与Roy Captain White在美国华盛顿州克拉克溫哥華结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她于1905年住在美国纽约州奧瑟格德卡度。她去世于1994年11月28日在美国华盛顿州可利吉塔特懷特 賽門享年93岁，埋葬在美国华盛顿州斯卡馬尼亞卡森。

nameMeanings.name: Covey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: shortened form of MacCovey, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Cobhthaigh (see Coffey ).

English: unexplained. Covey is used of ‘pantry’ in the early modern period and covey ‘little chap’ (a diminutive of cove, Middle English cof ‘bold, eager, courageous’; see Cove ) in the early nineteenth century, but there is no evidence to confirm either as the source for the surname.

variants.name: Coffey
meanings: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Cathbhadha ‘descendant of Cathbhadh’, a byname meaning ‘battle tent’, Ó Cathbhuadhaigh ‘descendant of Cathbhuadhach’ (‘battle victorious’), or Ó Cathmhogha ‘descendant of Cathmhugh’ (‘battle slave’).
shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Cobhthaigh ‘descendant of Cobhthach’, a byname meaning ‘victorious’.

nameMeanings.name: Ruth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name (of uncertain derivation) of a Moabite woman who left her own people to remain with her mother-in-law Naomi, and afterwards became the wife of Boaz and an ancestress of David. Her story is told in the book of the Bible that bears her name. It was used among the Puritans in England in the 16th century, partly because of its association with the English vocabulary word ruth meaning ‘compassion’. It has always been popular as a Jewish name, but is now also widespread among people of many different cultures and creeds.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

historicEvents.title: So Much Farm Land
historicEvents.description: A law that funded many irrigation and agricultural projects in the western states.

historicEvents.title: Pike's Market
historicEvents.description: Pike’s Market is one of the oldest still working farmer’s markets in the US. It is located in Seattle’s central business district, just north of Belltown , and southwest of central waterfront and Elliott Bay. One of the attractions there is the gum wall.
Charles Lindbergh makes the first solo nonstop transatlantic flight in his plane The Spirit of St. Louis.

Galloping Gertie is the reference used to describe the Tacoma Narrows Bridge. It opened on July 1, 1940 four months later it no longer existed. On November 7, 1940 the wind gusts came up to 40 miles an hour causing the bridge to twist and vibrate violently before it collapsed into Puget Sound. The only victim of the bridge collapsing was a three-legged paralyzed dog named Tubby whose owner tried to rescue him from the car but he wouldn’t go with him.
United States military forces play a leading role against North Korean and Chinese troops in Korean War.

historicEvents.title: Seattle Space Needle is Built
historicEvents.description: Construction on the Seattle Space Needle was constructed on April 17, 1961, and was opened on April 21, 1962. It was built for the World’s Fair in 1962. The space needle is an observation tower that is iconic to Seattle and a landmark of the Pacific Northwest. It was meant to only be lite for 75 nights per year or less to reduce light pollution. On September 11, 2001 the lights of the space were left on for 11 days straight.

historicEvents.title: The President Resigns
historicEvents.description: From the aftermath of the Watergate Scandal, Richard Nixon resigns. Being the first president to do so.

Dedicated on November 17-21, 1980 by President Spencer W. Kimball, the Seattle Washington Temple for the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints was the first temple built in the Pacific Northwest and in Washington. It is also only one of five temples where the angel Moroni is holding the gold plates.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Temples of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints- Seattle Washington Temple

en.lifeSummary: When Ruth Mary Covey was born on 12 October 1901, in Otsego, Otsego, New York, United States, her father, Silvanus Hartwell Covey, was 43 and her mother, Carrie A Utter, was 48. She married Roy Captain White on 15 August 1925, in Vancouver, Clark, Washington, United States. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She lived in Decatur, Otsego, New York, United States in 1905. She died on 28 November 1994, in White Salmon, Klickitat, Washington, United States, at the age of 93, and was buried in Carson, Skamania, Washington, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ruth Mary Covey est née le 12 octobre 1901, à Otsego, Otsego, New York, États-Unis, son père, Silvanus Hartwell Covey, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Carrie A Utter, avait 48 ans. Elle a épousé Roy Captain White le 15 août 1925, à Vancouver, Clark, Washington, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle a vécu à Decatur, Otsego, New York, États-Unis en 1905. Elle est décédée le 28 novembre 1994, à White Salmon, Klickitat, Washington, États-Unis, à l’âge de 93 ans, et a été enterrée à Carson, Skamania, Washington, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ruth Mary Covey am 12. Oktober 1901 in Otsego, Otsego, New York, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Silvanus Hartwell Covey 43 und ihre Mutter Carrie A Utter 48. Sie hat am 15. August 1925 in Vancouver, Clark, Washington, Vereinigte Staaten Roy Captain White geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1905 hat sie in Decatur, Otsego, New York, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Sie starb am 28. November 1994 im Alter von 93 Jahren in White Salmon, Klickitat, Washington, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Carson, Skamania, Washington, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ruth Mary Covey è nata il il 12 ottobre 1901 nel Otsego, Otsego, New York, Stati Uniti d'America, sua padre, Silvanus Hartwell Covey, aveva 43 anni e sua madre, Carrie A Utter, aveva 48 anni. Lei sposato Roy Captain White il 15 agosto 1925, nel Vancouver, Clark, Washington, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Vissuto a Decatur, Otsego, New York, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1905. Morì il 28 novembre 1994, nel White Salmon, Klickitat, Washington, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 93 anni, e fu sepolta a Carson, Skamania, Washington, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Ruth Mary Coveyが1901年10月12日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州オトセゴオトセゴで生まれた時，父Silvanus Hartwell Coveyは43歳，母Carrie A Utterは48歳であった。1925年8月15日にアメリカ合衆国ワシントン州クラークヴァンコウバーでRoy Captain Whiteと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1905年にはアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州オトセゴデカトゥルに住んでいた。彼女は1994年11月28日にアメリカ合衆国ワシントン州クリクイタトウィト サルモンで93歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ワシントン州スカマニアカーソンにて埋葬された。

ko.White Salmon, Klickitat, Washington, Uni: 미국 워싱턴 주 클릭이타트 휘트 살몬
ko.White Salmon, , Washington: 미국 워싱턴 주 클릭이타트 휘트 살몬
ko.lifeSummary: Ruth Mary Covey(이)가 1901년 10월 12일 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고 츠고에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Silvanus Hartwell Covey(은)는 43세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Carrie A Utter(은)는 48세였다. 그녀는 1925년 8월 15일 미국 워싱턴 주 클라르크 버코우버에서 Roy Captain White(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1905년도에 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고 데카투르에서 살았다. 그녀는 1994년 11월 28일 미국 워싱턴 주 클릭이타트 휘트 살몬에서 93세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 워싱턴 주 스카먼이아 카슨에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ruth Mary Covey nasceu em 12 de outubro de 1901, em Otsego, Otsego, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Silvanus Hartwell Covey, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Carrie A Utter, tinha 48 anos. Ela casou-se com Roy Captain White em 15 de agosto de 1925, em Vancouver, Clark, Washington, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela viveu em Decatur, Otsego, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América em 1905. Ela faleceu em 28 de novembro de 1994, em White Salmon, Klickitat, Washington, Estados Unidos da América, com 93 anos, e foi sepultada em Carson, Skamania, Washington, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.White Salmon, , Washington: уит салмон, кликкитат, Вашингтон, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ruth Mary Covey родилась 12 октября 1901 г. в местности оцего, оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Silvanus Hartwell Covey было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Carrie A Utter, 48. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Roy Captain White 15 августа 1925 г. в местности ванкоувир, кларк, Вашингтон, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она жила в местности декатур, оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты в 1905 году. Она умерла 28 ноября 1994 г. в местности уит салмон, кликкитат, Вашингтон, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 93 лет и была похоронена в местности карсон, скаманя, Вашингтон, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ruth Mary Covey nació el 12 de octubre de 1901, en Otsego, Otsego, Nueva York, Estados Unidos, su papá, Silvanus Hartwell Covey, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Carrie A Utter, tenía 48 años. Se casó con Roy Captain White el 15 de agosto de 1925, en Vancouver, Clark, Washington, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Vivió en Decatur, Otsego, Nueva York, Estados Unidos en 1905. Murió el 28 de noviembre de 1994, en White Salmon, Klickitat, Washington, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 93 años, y fue sepultada en Carson, Skamania, Washington, Estados Unidos.
